original_hypothesis.title: ERC20Gauges._decrementWeightUntilFree Infinite Loop When User Has Zero-Weight Gauge
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In ERC20Gauges._decrementWeightUntilFree(), the loop counter `i` is only incremented inside the `if (userGaugeWeight != 0)` block. If a user has a gauge with 0 weight in their gauge set (added via incrementGauge(gauge, 0)), and the loop reaches that gauge before freeing enough weight, it will loop infinitely on the zero-weight entry, causing an out-of-gas revert. This blocks all token transfers and burns for the affected user.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The incrementGauge function does not validate weight > 0, allowing a gauge to be added to the user's set with 0 weight. The greedy decrement algorithm assumes all gauges in the set have positive weight.

proof_of_signal.pattern: unchecked { ++i; } only inside if (userGaugeWeight != 0) block; zero-weight gauge causes infinite iteration

preconditions: User calls incrementGauge(gauge, 0), adding gauge to their set with 0 weight
User has other gauge allocations that consume their balance
User attempts to transfer or burn tokens requiring weight freeing

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Loop counter increment inside conditional block
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: The for loop at line 484 only increments i (line 498) when userGaugeWeight != 0. A gauge with 0 weight causes the same index to be checked repeatedly, creating an infinite loop.
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No weight > 0 check in incrementGauge; no unconditional i++ in loop

analysis_questions: Can an attacker cause this on the SurplusGuildMinter if mintRatio is set to 0?
Is incrementGauge(gauge, 0) actually reachable given GuildToken override checks?
Can the user recover by manually decrementing the zero-weight gauge?
validation_requirements: Test incrementGauge(gauge, 0) succeeds and adds to gauge set
Test subsequent transfer/burn reverts with out of gas
Verify if decrementGauge(gauge, 0) can remove the problematic entry

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: User calls GuildToken.incrementGauge(gauge, 0). The gauge is added to their _userGauges set with 0 weight. Later, when the user tries to transfer tokens that require weight freeing, _decrementWeightUntilFree enters an infinite loop on the zero-weight gauge, consuming all gas.
prerequisites: User (or protocol acting on user's behalf) calls incrementGauge with weight=0

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: User's GUILD tokens permanently locked - cannot transfer or burn
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Self-inflicted for direct users; could affect SurplusGuildMinter if mintRatio=0
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: User may recover by calling decrementGauge(gauge, 0) to remove the entry

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for incrementGauge with zero weight followed by transfer

technical_investigation_focus.gas_analysis_needed: Loop will consume all gas in block
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: User transactions revert with out of gas

validation_result.mechanism: infinite loop on zero-weight gauge entry
validation_result.impact_summary: If a user has a gauge with 0 weight in their _userGauges set, _decrementWeightUntilFree will loop infinitely on that entry, causing out-of-gas reverts and blocking transfers/burns for the affected user.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The _decrementWeightUntilFree function in ERC20Gauges.sol contains a for-loop where the loop counter `i` is only incremented inside the `if (userGaugeWeight != 0)` block. When a gauge has 0 weight, the entire if-block is skipped, meaning `i` is never incremented. The loop condition `i < size && (userFreeWeight + userFreed) < weight` remains true because `i` doesn't advance and no weight is freed. This causes an infinite loop consuming all gas.

The precondition of having a 0-weight gauge in a user's set is achievable because:
1. incrementGauge(gauge, 0) adds the gauge to _userGauges[user] via `_userGauges[user].add(gauge)` (idempotent add)
2. getUserGaugeWeight[user][gauge] += 0 remains 0
3. No validation prevents weight=0 in incrementGauge or _incrementGaugeWeight
4. SurplusGuildMinter.stake() with mintRatio=0 would call incrementGauge(term, 0)

The user CAN recover by calling decrementGauge(gauge, 0), which removes the 0-weight gauge from the set (since oldWeight == weight triggers the removal). However, this is still a real code defect causing DoS.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal is accurate. The `unchecked { ++i; }` at the end of the loop body is strictly inside the `if (userGaugeWeight != 0)` block. When `userGaugeWeight == 0`, the loop index `i` is never incremented, causing infinite iteration on the same array index.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The unchecked { ++i; } is inside the if (userGaugeWeight != 0) block. When a gauge has 0 weight, i never increments, causing the loop to repeatedly check the same index forever.
No check prevents weight=0. When weight=0, the gauge is added to _userGauges[user] via .add(gauge) but getUserGaugeWeight stays 0, creating the precondition for the infinite loop.

calling_sequence: 1. User calls GuildToken.incrementGauge(gauge, 0) which calls ERC20Gauges.incrementGauge(gauge, 0)
3. _incrementGaugeWeight adds gauge to _userGauges[user] via .add(gauge), weight stays 0
4. _incrementUserAndGlobalWeights adds 0 to getUserWeight, passes overweight check
5. Later, user calls transfer() or _burn() which triggers _decrementWeightUntilFree(user, amount)
6. The for-loop encounters the 0-weight gauge, enters infinite loop since ++i is inside if(userGaugeWeight != 0)
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The entry point is incrementGauge(gauge, 0) at ERC20Gauges.sol:207. This calls _incrementGaugeWeight at line 221, which adds the gauge to _userGauges[user] at line 226 without checking weight > 0. getUserGaugeWeight[user][gauge] stays 0 after += 0 at line 231. When the user later needs to free weight (e.g., during transfer at ERC20Gauges.sol:459 which calls _decrementWeightUntilFree), the loop at line 479 hits the 0-weight gauge. Since userGaugeWeight == 0, the if-block at line 485 is skipped entirely, and ++i at line 498 is never reached.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that are relevant to this issue. The vulnerability is in the base ERC20Gauges code independent of deployment configuration.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The scenario is valid: (1) A user (or SurplusGuildMinter with mintRatio=0) calls incrementGauge(gauge, 0), adding a 0-weight gauge to _userGauges. (2) The user has other gauge allocations consuming their balance. (3) When the user tries to transfer tokens requiring weight freeing, _decrementWeightUntilFree enters an infinite loop on the 0-weight gauge entry, consuming all gas. The user's tokens are effectively locked until they call decrementGauge(gauge, 0) to remove the problematic entry.
validation_result.impact_assessment: MEDIUM severity. The defect causes a self-inflicted DoS where affected users cannot transfer or burn their GUILD tokens until they manually remove the 0-weight gauge via decrementGauge(gauge, 0). This could also affect the SurplusGuildMinter contract if mintRatio is set to 0 by governance, as stake() would call incrementGauge with 0 weight. Recovery is possible but requires awareness of the issue.
recommended_mitigations: Move `unchecked { ++i; }` outside the `if (userGaugeWeight != 0)` block so the loop index always increments regardless of weight
Add a `require(weight != 0)` check in incrementGauge() or _incrementGaugeWeight() to prevent adding 0-weight gauges
In SurplusGuildMinter.stake(), add a `require(guildAmount != 0)` check before calling incrementGauge
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy GuildToken with all dependencies. 2. Mint GUILD to a test user. 3. Add a gauge. 4. User calls incrementGauge(gauge, 100e18) to allocate weight. 5. User calls incrementGauge(anotherGauge, 0) to add a 0-weight gauge. 6. Enable transfers on GuildToken. 7. User tries to transfer tokens requiring weight freeing. 8. Observe out-of-gas revert. 9. User calls decrementGauge(anotherGauge, 0) to remove 0-weight entry. 10. User retries transfer, succeeds.
uncertainties: The exact ordering of gauges in EnumerableSet.values() determines whether the infinite loop is triggered before or after enough weight is freed - if the 0-weight gauge happens to be at the end of the array and enough weight is freed before reaching it, the loop terminates normally
Whether governance would realistically set mintRatio to 0 in SurplusGuildMinter
additional_testing_needed: Verify that decrementGauge(gauge, 0) successfully removes the 0-weight entry as a recovery mechanism
Test SurplusGuildMinter.stake() behavior when mintRatio=0 to confirm it creates the problematic 0-weight gauge entry
Test whether the GuildToken override of _decrementGaugeWeight adds any additional complications (e.g., debt ceiling checks) for the 0-weight case
